seoTitle: Plumpton vs Point Cook — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Plumpton and Point Cook. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Plumpton vs Point Cook: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Plumpton and Point Cook.
heading: Plumpton vs Point Cook — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Plumpton, Victoria and Point Cook, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Plumpton has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Point Cook (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Plumpton or Point Cook safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Plumpton has a flood risk score of 57/100, while Point Cook scores 46/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Plumpton scores 17/100 for bushfire risk. Point Cook scores 49/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Plumpton or Point Cook?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Plumpton has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.7% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
